humanities_role_explanation: Evaluates LLM capabilities on translating and summarizing historical 16th-century correspondence written in Latin and Early New High German.
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Text-based evaluation using 16th-century letters written in Latin and Early New High German with translation and summarization targets in modern German and English.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Evaluates machine translation of historical languages (Latin and Early New High German) into modern languages, as well as cross-lingual summarization of paragraph-level historical documents.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: The evaluation is highly specific to 16th-century Latin and Early New High German correspondence.

llm_is_primary_system.note: GPT-4 is the primary system evaluated for translation and summarization performance.

arts_humanities_data.note: Classics, Language and Linguistics, and History (16th-century Swiss Reformation correspondence).

quantitative_metrics.note: BLEU, ChrF, and manual human evaluation scores (evaluating coverage of names, times/events, completeness, and correctness).

english_language.note: The paper is written in English.

summary: This paper evaluates the performance of GPT-4 on machine translation and paragraph-level summarization of 16th-century letters written in Latin and Early New High German. It demonstrates that LLMs represent a massive leap forward in translation quality for low-resource historical languages compared to traditional neural machine translation services.
key_findings: GPT-4 achieves a BLEU score of 27.07 for Latin-to-German and 34.50 for Latin-to-English translation, outperforming Google Translate by approximately 10 BLEU points in both directions. Human evaluation of GPT-4 summaries shows high performance in extracting names, times, and events accurately without hallucinating.
paper_name: LLM-based Machine Translation and Summarization for Latin
